justification: All 5 rubric problems identified: (1) connection pool math performed explicitly (8×12=96 of 100), (2) synchronous call chain identified as 4-hop cascading failure risk, (3) Redis SPOF identified, (4) shared CI/CD identified as defeating microservices independence, (5) NFS mount identified as hidden shared dependency with hang risk. Prioritized phased remediation plan provided across 4 phases. Trade-offs documented for each major architectural decision with alternatives considered.

observations.text: Connection pool math explicitly calculated (96 of 100) with concrete mitigation to reduce per-service pool to 10
NFS hang risk specifically called out — not just I/O contention but the insidious NFS hang behavior where services block on writes
Shared CI/CD correctly identified as eliminating independent deployability, the primary benefit of microservices
Phased remediation plan is well-sequenced: immediate risk reduction first, then architectural improvements, avoiding big-bang recommendations
Trade-offs section is thorough with gains, sacrifices, and alternatives for each decision
Identified additional valid issues beyond the 5 required: schema coupling, missing observability, absence of resilience patterns
Synchronous chain described as 4-hop rather than 5-hop — minor discrepancy but the core risk analysis (latency multiplication, cascading failure) is correct

Chain depth counted as 4 hops instead of the expected 5 (Gateway->Order->Courier->Carrier->Notification) — the scenario counts Gateway as the first hop while MJ appears to count it differently, though all services in the chain are identified

agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"This architecture exhibits several critical anti-patterns that will compound under load and organizational growth. The most severe issues are: (1) a single PostgreSQL instance shared by 8 microservices creates a distributed monolith with a shared database, negating the core benefit of microservices; (2) the connection pool is at 96% capacity with zero headroom; (3) a 4-hop synchronous call chain creates cascading failure risk and latency amplification; (4) the shared Redis
